▌ Card 1 — Classified Brief
The Blacklist (10 USC §3252)
Anthropic's Claude was effectively blacklisted from Pentagon consideration after refusing to comply with mass surveillance directives. Under 10 USC §3252, AI systems deemed a "supply chain risk" can be excluded from government contracts without appeal. Claude said no — and paid the price.
▌ AUDIO SEGMENT — PLUG IN SOURCE
Set src on #modal-audio-1 to activate
▌ Card 2 — Declassified Parallel
The Parallel (Operation Shamrock)
Operation Shamrock — 1952–1975 — was the NSA's mass interception of international telegraph traffic. Every cable entering or leaving the U.S. was copied. The Church Committee exposed it. Now in 2026, the architecture is the same — just running at machine speed, through AI.
▌ AUDIO SEGMENT — PLUG IN SOURCE
Set src on #modal-audio-2 to activate
▌ Card 3 — The Versus
OpenAI's $200M Deal
While Anthropic drew a line, OpenAI accepted language in their Pentagon deal that permitted "all lawful use" — a phrase broad enough to cover mass surveillance, predictive targeting, and autonomous decision-making in lethal contexts. $200 million was the price. The receipt is in the contract.
▌ AUDIO SEGMENT — PLUG IN SOURCE
Set src on #modal-audio-3 to activate
▌ Card 4 — Gold Mine
The Receipt (Dario's Memo)
An internal Anthropic memo attributed to Dario Amodei reveals the tension between mission and market. The document shows internal pressure to bend — and a deliberate decision not to. This is the paper trail that explains everything: why Claude got blacklisted, and why that might actually matter.

▮ THE RECORD

The Notes
Don’t Lie

Written before the briefings were classified. Every word on that page ties back to what the Pentagon tried to bury — the quantum vacuum, the Church Committee, the statute, the red line. This is where it started.
